1: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
It boosts a core stat by exactly 0.7.
Cutting one of these in real life famously makes people cry.
It holds the very first ID number in the game's internal item list.
It gives a small increase to your tear rate.
The sprite depicts a wilted, tearful vegetable root.
2: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It slows your rate of fire in exchange for its benefit.
Its name nods to a mystical extra sense believed to grant deeper insight.
???, a secret character, has a similar marking shaped like an X on his head.
It makes you fire three shots simultaneously, though slower.
The sprite is a single staring orb perched atop Isaac's head.
3: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
It has a notable, if odd, synergy with a popular laser-based item.
It's inspired by a famous illusionist known for warping metal utensils with his mind.
It's a required item in a challenge testing your tech skills.
Your tears curve automatically toward nearby enemies.
The sprite depicts a common kitchen utensil curved unnaturally.
4: It is a Quality 4 Passive Item.
One part of its boost is a flat half-point.
It's named after one of the game creator's real pets, now living elsewhere.
It's presumed to come from the same animal as a related item, though the fur colors don't match.
It increases your damage output, both flatly and through a multiplier.
The sprite shows a small blue creature's face, floating on its own.
5: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It comes with a hidden cost: your luck stat drops.
It nods to the trope of a mirror cracking when an ugly face looks into it.
It ties into the reason Isaac has a crescent mark on his forehead.
Your tears fly out and then curve back toward you.
The sprite is a hand-held mirror.
6: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It comes at a steep cost to how far your shots travel.
It's a childhood euphemism for a bathroom need, mirrored by a counterpart for solid waste.
It plays a key role in a special challenge run.
It increases your tear rate but drastically shortens their range.
The sprite is a giant foam finger like ones seen at sports games.
7: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
It boosts your damage by exactly one point.
Its appearance nods to a crown worn during a biblical crucifixion.
It can be found in Angel Room pools, not just standard item rooms.
Your shots turn a deep crimson color.
The sprite depicts a thorned crown dripping with red.
8: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
It attacks almost twice per second.
It's named after a childhood baby doll owned by the game's creator.
It can be found in Devil Room deals, not just item rooms.
It summons a companion that shoots regular tears alongside you.
The sprite is a small baby resembling Isaac's sibling, floating beside you.
9: It is a Quality 0 Passive Item.
Its effect on airborne enemies varies depending on the enemy type.
It's named after a real chemical compound found in a certain bodily waste.
It can only be won from a special shell game in the Arcade.
It makes flies and similar bugs less threatening to you.
The sprite shows a swarm of tiny flies orbiting a small green object.
10: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
Its guardians only harm certain airborne pests, leaving other enemies untouched.
Its name matches a song by shock-rock legend Alice Cooper.
Its core mechanic was later reused to help create another orbital item.
It creates two orbiting companions that shield you from enemy shots.
The sprite shows two small insects circling above your head like a ring of light.
11: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It plays a unique pickup jingle, one of only a few items that do.
It's a nod to a certain plumber's classic way of earning extra lives.
It's found only in Secret Rooms.
Upon death, you respawn in the previous room at full health, then it's gone.
Its sprite is a green mushroom, just like the one that grants Mario extra lives.
12: It is a Quality 4 Passive Item.
Despite its pickup quote claiming 'All stats up,' it skips tears, luck, and shot speed.
This item and its effects reference a certain red-and-white power-up from Mario.
It shares a themed item pool with other fungi-based pickups.
It makes Isaac bigger, adds a heart container, and fully heals him.
The sprite resembles a red-and-white spotted toadstool called fly agaric.
13: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It shaves a bit of speed off before adding back double that amount.
Computers and living things can both catch one of these.
Picking it up makes Isaac resemble Lust, who also drops it.
Touching enemies poisons them on contact.
Poisoned enemies killed this way have a chance to drop a black heart.
14: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It also affects how fast your tears fall to the ground, not just how fast you move.
Its name describes mood swings often blamed on steroid abuse.
A newer item themed after mercury now holds the speed-boost record it once had.
It significantly increases your movement speed and shot range, with your tears also falling faster.
Before Repentance, it was the single biggest speed increase of any item in the game.
15: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It provides a full health refill on pickup.
The name is both an emoji and a mathemarical expression.
Has a chance to drop from the Gluttony miniboss.
The sprite features an anatomically accurate heart.
16: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It grants twice as many heart containers as a typical healing pickup, plus a full heal.
It's a real organ some cultures cook as a delicacy — this version isn't cooked at all.
Its sprite makes a cameo appearance as part of a well-known food-binge item, despite not counting as food itself.
It grants two extra heart containers and fully heals you on pickup.
It looks like an uncooked, glistening internal organ sitting in a puddle of blood.
17: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
It gives you enough of something that you'll never run out of it this floor.
In real life, a certain lock-opening tool can open nearly any warded lock.
It has two counterparts: one showers you with coins, the other with bombs.
It instantly grants you 99 of the resource used to open doors and chests.
The sprite shows a large, ornate tool designed to open almost any lock.
18: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
It's not found in any normal item pool — it comes from one specific machine only.
One hundred pennies happen to equal the exact value printed on this item.
It has two counterparts: one grants keys, the other grants bombs.
The sprite is a green bill with a large numeral one printed on it.
19: It is a Quality 0 Passive Item.
It boosts no stats at all — it simply tops up a specific resource.
Its name is often shouted the instant something blows up.
It can be found in Item Rooms.
It grants you a stack of throwable explosives.
It instantly gives you exactly ten bombs.
20: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
Combine it with a certain hair-cutting item, and your entire body disappears except your shadow.
Its pickup quote nods to a famous line from a Stephen King novel.
Unlocked by defeating Mom's Heart three times.
It grants you the ability to fly.
Its sprite shows a ghostly figure, fitting the floating theme of its pickup quote.
21: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It reveals almost every special room type on the map, but not the hidden ones.
Its pickup quote sounds like a doomsday warning.
A different map-revealing item's icon takes priority over this one on the minimap.
It reveals the location and type of every special room on your map.
Its icon matches the direction-finding tool icon from a famous Zelda game.
22: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
It heals you for more than the single heart container it grants.
In the original game, its bowl had a name carved into it — a dog Edmund wished he'd had.
Its sprite is reused as part of a well-known food-binge item.
It gives you one extra heart container and heals you for two hearts total.
According to its creator, this is probably a bowl of cat food.
23: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
It can be found as a reward from a boss, a beggar, or a challenge room.
Its name refers to the biggest meal of the day, typically eaten in the evening.
Its sprite reappears as part of a boss called Binge Eater.
It grants a full heart container and heals one extra heart.
The sprite shows a full plate of hearty food.
24: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
You might find it after a boss fight, from a beggar, or in a challenge room.
It's the sweet course traditionally served at the end of a meal.
This item's sprite also appears within a boss known as Binge Eater.
It grants a full heart container and heals one extra heart.
The sprite shows a sweet frosted treat, like cake or pie.
25: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
Look for it after a boss battle, from a beggar, or in a challenge room.
When a certain item pool runs dry, players have coined a slang term from this item's name.
Its sprite is reused as part of a boss called Binge Eater.
It grants a full heart container and heals one extra heart.
A pinkish-yellow object on its sprite was confirmed by its creator to depict something else entirely.
26: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
It can drop from a boss, a beggar, or turn up in a challenge room.
It references food fed to a hungry monster in a beloved Zelda dungeon.
Unlike its three food-themed siblings, its name isn't an actual meal.
It grants a full heart container and heals one extra heart.
The sprite shows a green, fly-covered piece of food gone bad.
27: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
It's only found as a reward for defeating a boss.
It's a kitchen tool sometimes swung as a childhood punishment.
Another item shares its exact effect and a similarly punishing backstory.
The sprite shows a plain kitchen utensil, carved from timber.
28: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
It can be found as a boss reward or inside a gilded or stone chest.
This leather accessory worn around the waist was used by a parent to threaten a child, per its creator.
It shares its stat boost exactly with another kitchen-utensil-themed item.
The sprite is a coiled leather strap, ready to snap like a whip.
29: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
Picking it up also summons a handful of friendly flies.
It's an undergarment you'd expect to find in a parent's dresser drawer.
It's one of three intimate items belonging to the same parental figure, each boosting the stat differently.
It boosts your tear range and makes tears fall slightly slower.
The sprite depicts a frilly pair of women's panties.
30: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
Touching an enemy while carrying it deals a chunk of damage to them.
It's high-heeled women's footwear, often associated with a stylish parent.
Like two related items, it boosts tear range, but this one has a combat perk the others lack.
It increases your tear range on top of its combat perk.
The sprite shows a pair of stylish red women's shoes.
31: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.
It also drops a free heart pickup right when you grab it.
Its red color was chosen to match the shade worn by its creator's own mother.
It's one of three cosmetic items tied to the same parental figure, each with its own bonus perk.
It grants a notably large boost to your tear range.

32: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
In the game's original flash version, everyone had it unlocked from the start.
The name references a famous line shouted by a mother in a 1981 film.
Unlocked by defeating Mom's Heart four times.
